methods.description: Collection of airborne material using Burkard multi-vial cyclones at specific rural and urban sites in Worcestershire, UK, over multiple years.

steps.description: Airborne material was collected with two Burkard multi-vial cyclones.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect airborne material using Burkard multi-vial cyclones.

steps.description: Collection performed at two sites in Worcestershire, UK; one rural site (52.2544°, − 2.2537°) and one urban (52.1969°, − 2.2422°).
steps.searchable_summary: Define collection sites with geographic coordinates.

steps.description: Material was collected for 10 weeks from the beginning of July during the years 2016-2018 (Hanson et al., 2022), hence providing 420 daily samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect samples daily for 10 weeks starting in July for years 2016, 2017, 2018.

methods.description: Processing collected airborne material samples to extract DNA, pool samples, and perform Illumina MiSEQ sequencing targeting the ITS2 region.

steps.description: DNA was extracted according to (Hanson et al., (2022).
steps.searchable_summary: Extract DNA from airborne material samples using a standard protocol.

steps.description: Extracted DNA was pooled into 6 annual aggregations for each of the years 2016-2018.
steps.searchable_summary: Pool DNA samples annually for each site.
outputs: 6 pooled annual DNA samples (2 sites * 3 years)

steps.description: Samples were profiled using the ITS2 region.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare DNA samples for sequencing by targeting the ITS2 region.

steps.description: Illumina MiSEQ sequencing was performed externally by Eurofins Genomics.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Illumina MiSEQ sequencing on prepared DNA samples.

methods.method_name: Bioinformatic and Statistical Analysis of Sequencing Data
methods.description: Analysis of raw sequencing data to identify pollen taxa, calculate diversity metrics (alpha and beta), and perform statistical tests to compare sites.

steps.description: Bioinformatic analysis was performed in R.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up R environment for bioinformatic analysis.

steps.description: Using Dada2, phyloseq and vegan packages as detailed in Hanson et al. (2022)
steps.searchable_summary: Process raw sequencing data into ASV table and taxonomic table using Dada2, phyloseq, and vegan R packages.

steps.description: Taxonomic assignment against the UNITE eukaryotic database v8.3
steps.searchable_summary: Assign taxonomy to ASVs using the UNITE database.

steps.description: Postprocessing of the Amplicon Sequence Variant (ASV) data for pollen producing plants (Anthophyta and Coniferophyta) was done by c-shell scripting.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter ASV data to include only pollen producing plants (Anthophyta, Coniferophyta) using c-shell scripts.

steps.description: The most prevalent pollen producing plants at each site were determined at the genus level by examining the top ten taxa.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify top 10 most prevalent pollen genera at each site from filtered ASV data.
outputs: list of top 10 genera per site

steps.description: Total biodiversity was calculated by enumerating all possible taxa at the genus level.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate total number of unique pollen genera found in the filtered ASV data.

steps.description: ASV data were used to calculate alpha and beta diversities in a similar way as for spores in the study by Hanson et al. (2022).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate alpha and beta diversity metrics from filtered ASV data.

steps.description: For beta diversity we use nonmetric multidimensional scaling (NMDS) based on Bray-Curtis dissimilarity to study how different the rural and urban sites are in terms of pollen flora.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform NMDS analysis using Bray-Curtis dissimilarity on filtered ASV data.

steps.description: Shannon and Simpson (1-D) alphadiversity indices were used to examine overall pollen diversity and evenness
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Shannon and Simpson (1-D) alpha diversity indices from filtered ASV data.

steps.description: A t test was applied to plant ASV data agglomerated at genus taxonomical level to examine whether there was a statistically significant difference in the number of plant genera at each site.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform independent t-test to compare the number of plant genera between sites using agglomerated ASV data.
